name: Bollywood Club Night

interaction_intensity.label: Group-Centric
interaction_intensity.description: High energy on the floor. While groups are common, solo attendees can easily blend into the collective choreography.

seasonal_context.peak_note: Diwali and Holi themed nights see peak attendance and 'Premium Ticket' requirements.
seasonal_context.off_peak_note: Standard monthly socials are more predictable and spacious.

budget_tips: Group-sharing a 'Pitcher' or bottle (if safe) is often more cost-effective than individual complex cocktails.
Check if your ticket includes a 'Welcome Drink' to save on your first round.
social_dynamics: Performative and celebratory; heavy focus on dancing and fashion.
social_expectations: Expressive dancing, group energy, and fashion-forward presentation.
solo_tips: The dance floor 'circle' is where the action is—standing on the fringe lets you watch before jumping in.
Complimenting someone’s outfit is the fastest way to make a 'night-only' friend.

safety_factors.label: Moderate Risk
safety_factors.description: Large crowds and high-decibel music; standard nightlife precautions apply.

variants.name: Bhangra/Choreo Workshop Night
variants.occurrence_note: A pre-party session where everyone learns the hook-step together.

physical_proximity: High; vibrant and crowded dance floors.
recommendations: Look for themed nights (e.g., Holi or Diwali) for the best experience
Stay near the lighting booth for the best view of the dancers

name: EDM Club

interaction_intensity.label: High Pressure
interaction_intensity.description: The 'current' of the dance floor is strong; standing still can feel socially awkward in high-traffic zones.

seasonal_context.peak_note: Holiday weekends and 'Guest DJ' nights often trigger mandatory coat-checks and higher drink minimums.
seasonal_context.off_peak_note: Local resident nights usually have waived covers before 11 PM.

budget_tips: Identify the 'Free Water' station immediately; bottled water here can cost as much as a beer.
Avoid 'Table Service' zones to prevent incidental 'minimum spend' pressure.
social_expectations: Focus is primarily on the DJ; verbal conversation is minimal on the dance floor.
solo_tips: Ear protection is essential for maintaining solo comfort.

safety_factors.label: High Risk
safety_factors.description: High sensory intensity and crowd-crush potential during headliner sets.

physical_proximity: Constant incidental contact; very high density on the main floor.
recommendations: Locate the free water station immediately

name: Rave / Electronic Music Event

interaction_intensity.label: Music-First
interaction_intensity.description: The focus is on the sound and light production. Socializing is optional and often happens in 'chill' side-rooms.

seasonal_context.peak_note: Large-scale 'Massives' see significant price hikes in transport and hydration.
seasonal_context.off_peak_note: Warehouse-style local raves often have lower overhead and consistent pricing.

budget_tips: Cash is often faster but cards are safer; bring a small amount of cash for 'emergency hydration'.
Pre-game your electrolytes to avoid the 'Festival Tax' on sports drinks.
social_expectations: PLUR (Peace, Love, Unity, Respect) culture; high acceptance of solo dancers and unique fashion.
solo_tips: Flow toys (like LED hoops) are a great non-verbal way to exist in the space.
Check the 'set times' so you don't burn out before the headliner.

safety_factors.label: High Sensory Risk
safety_factors.description: Extreme volume and strobe lighting; potential for dehydration in high-density areas.

physical_proximity: Variable; tight on the floor, but usually spacious at the perimeter.
recommendations: Hydrate at least 20oz of water per hour of dancing

name: Top 40 / Mainstream Club

interaction_intensity.label: Socially Charged
interaction_intensity.description: Highly performative; there is often a 'see and be seen' atmosphere that can feel isolating for solo guests.

seasonal_context.peak_note: New Year's Eve and Graduation season see the highest 'Surge' in cover charges and ride-share costs.
seasonal_context.off_peak_note: Thursday nights often offer 'No Cover' for locals or students.

budget_tips: The 'Surge' isn't just at the bar—budget 2x for your ride home during the 2 AM rush.
Stick to 'Well' drinks; 'Call' spirits are significantly marked up for the 'Top 40' crowd.
social_expectations: Group-heavy environment; dress codes are usually strictly enforced.
solo_tips: The bar is your safe haven; bartenders here are used to solo guests waiting for friends.
Look for clubs with a 'patio' area where the music is lower and conversation is easier.

safety_factors.label: Moderate-High Risk
safety_factors.description: High alcohol consumption and aggressive bouncers; keep a close eye on your drink.

physical_proximity: Extreme on the dance floor; high friction in bottle-service areas.
recommendations: Check the dress code on Instagram before going
Pre-book a ride home to avoid the 2 AM surge

name: K-Pop Night

interaction_intensity.label: Fan-Driven Social
interaction_intensity.description: Shared fandom lowers the barrier to entry. Expect people to dance in sync or cheer for specific music video moments.

seasonal_context.peak_note: New group 'Comeback' nights or major tour anniversaries drive higher attendance.
seasonal_context.off_peak_note: Regular fan-run nights are very stable and community-priced.

budget_tips: These events often have 'Freebies' (tradeables)—bring your own to participate without spending on merch.
Check if there's a 'Non-Alcoholic' discount; many K-Pop fans prioritize the dance floor over the bar.
social_expectations: High energy and mutual respect; often feels more like a convention than a dark club.
solo_tips: If you know the 'fan chants' or choreography, join in! It's the ultimate icebreaker.
Carry a lightstick if you have one; it’s a beacon for other solo fans.

safety_factors.label: Low Risk
safety_factors.description: Community-focused events with generally lower levels of aggressive intoxication.

variants.name: Choreo/Lightstick Meetup
variants.occurrence_note: Focuses on shared performance; fans are highly approachable.

physical_proximity: Moderate-High; coordinated group dancing requires some 'elbow room'.
recommendations: Check if there is a 'random play dance' segment
Bring tradeable fan-made items (freebies) to meet people

name: Afrobeats Night

interaction_intensity.label: Rhythmic Engagement
interaction_intensity.description: The music is designed for movement. Socializing is fluid and often happens through shared rhythm on the floor.

seasonal_context.peak_note: International artist tours significantly increase the entry price and 'Bottle Service' demand.
seasonal_context.off_peak_note: Local DJ nights are the best value for 'Vibe-to-Cost' ratio.

budget_tips: Arriving before midnight often saves 50% on the cover charge.
Dress codes are strictly enforced—don't lose your entry fee by wearing the wrong shoes.
social_expectations: High emphasis on style and 'vibe'; inclusive but high-energy.
solo_tips: Don't be afraid to dance alone; the music is the centerpiece and everyone is focused on the 'groove'.
Finding a spot near the DJ booth often puts you in the center of the best energy.

safety_factors.label: Standard Nightlife Risk
safety_factors.description: Crowded dance floors and dim lighting; standard awareness is required.

physical_proximity: High; constant rhythmic movement in close quarters.
recommendations: Arrive late—the vibe usually peaks after midnight

name: House / Techno Room

interaction_intensity.label: Internalized Focus
interaction_intensity.description: Very low pressure to interact. The steady 4/4 beat allows for a 'meditative' solo dance experience.

seasonal_context.peak_note: Underground events during 'Music Week' conferences see high volatility.
seasonal_context.off_peak_note: Sunday 'After-hours' or weekday minimal sets are the most 'Budget-Friendly'.

budget_tips: The focus is internal—you can comfortably 'nurse' a single high-quality drink for a long time.
Skip the fancy cocktails; the dark environment makes 'simple' drinks (Soda/Lime/Spirit) safer and cheaper.
social_expectations: Anonymity is respected; talking on the dance floor is often frowned upon.
solo_tips: The 'dark corner' or the rail right in front of the DJ is perfect for solo immersion.
Let the rhythm take over; no one is judging your moves in a techno room.

safety_factors.label: Controlled Environment
safety_factors.description: Often more 'serious' music fans; lower aggression but watch for fatigue from long sets.

physical_proximity: Moderate; people usually respect a 'personal bubble' for dancing.
recommendations: Check the lineup for specific sub-genres
Bring a fan for crowded, poorly ventilated rooms

name: Nightclub-Style Bar (Lounge)

interaction_intensity.label: Fluid Pressure
interaction_intensity.description: Easier than a club but more intense than a bar. You can choose to be a spectator at a table or a participant on the small floor.

seasonal_context.peak_note: Late-night transitions (after 11 PM) are when 'Bar' pricing shifts to 'Club' logic.
seasonal_context.off_peak_note: Early-evening 'Happy Hour' is the best time for solo reconnaissance.

budget_tips: Take advantage of 'No Cover' before the DJ starts to lock in a spot without the entry tax.
Use this as a 'Transition' spot; drink prices are usually 20% lower than full nightclubs.
social_expectations: A mix of 'chilling' and 'partying'; more 'mingle-friendly' than a standard club.
solo_tips: Claim a spot at the bar rail to maintain a 'home base' while watching the dance floor.
Great for 'club-light' energy without the commitment of a full venue.

safety_factors.label: Moderate Risk
safety_factors.description: High foot traffic in small spaces can lead to spills and accidental bumps.

physical_proximity: High near the bar and floor; moderate in seating areas.
recommendations: Look for venues with no cover charge
Great for a 'warm-up' before a bigger event

name: Sundown Day Party

interaction_intensity.label: Music-Focused
interaction_intensity.description: Conversation is optional; shared appreciation for the production is the primary driver.

social_expectations: Mature crowd; higher ratio of individuals there for the music than for 'hunting' social status.
solo_tips: Arrive early to enjoy the transition from daylight to sunset.
Position yourself near the sound booth for the best acoustic experience.

safety_factors.label: Low Risk
safety_factors.description: Daylight visibility and a more mature crowd generally lead to a safer environment.

budget_tips: Sunscreen and hydration are the hidden costs of day parties.
Check for 'Early Bird' tickets as these events often sell out by afternoon.
